candidate_summary: LoRA updates projected to null-space of pre-trained weights with continuous-time rubato alpha(t) schedule, breath-budget integral constraint, and cosine-annealed descending-tonic null-space target inspired by Romanian Doina melismatic free-rhythm improvisation.
content_words: null-space LoRA continuous-time melisma gradient rubato schedule
descending-tonic basin cosine-annealed minimum-energy null-space target
parlando rubato alpha-modulation per batch position gradient
sighing descent to tonic dor emotional

results.name: OPLoRA Orthogonal Projection LoRA
results.rationale: Orthogonal/null-space projection of LoRA updates to preserve pre-trained weights directly overlaps the null-space LoRA mechanism.

results.name: CLoRA Controlled Low-Rank Subspace
results.rationale: Subspace-constrained LoRA regularization shares the core idea of restricting LoRA updates to a controlled subspace.

results.name: O-LoRA Orthogonal Subspace Learning
results.rationale: Orthogonal subspace learning for LoRA aligns with the null-space projection component of the proposed mechanism.

results.name: Continual Gradient Low-Rank Projection
results.rationale: Gradient low-rank projection during fine-tuning overlaps with the null-space LoRA gradient-projection aspect.

results.name: Beyond Cosine Decay Infinite LR WSD
results.rationale: WSD continual pre-training schedule touches cosine-annealing concepts but lacks null-space projection or integral budget constraints.

results.name: WSD Warmup-Stable-Decay LR Scheduling
results.rationale: Generic LR schedule overlaps only weakly with the continuous-time rubato alpha(t) scheduling idea.

results.name: Dynamic LR Scheduling Loss-Changes
results.rationale: Loss-driven dynamic LR scheduling resembles gradient-SNR modulation but does not involve null-space or integral budget.

results.name: Linear Decay-to-Zero LR
results.rationale: Simple linear LR decay shares minimal conceptual surface with the proposed continuous-time rubato schedule.

decision_rationale: Four prior-art hits on the null-space/orthogonal-projection LoRA mechanism indicate substantial overlap with existing work, so the candidate fails novelty.
